call_transcript:
Agent: Hi Eleanor, this is Mark calling from Finance Solutions. I see you recently inquired about a personal loan on our website. Um, how are you doing today? 
Eleanor: Good, thanks. 
Agent: Great! So, based on your application, we are pre-approving you for a $10,000 Personal Loan at a rate of 8.99%. This could really help with, you know, consolidating debt or maybe a home improvement project? 
Eleanor: I was thinking more about a car. 
Agent: Excellent! A car is a great use of funds. Now, the monthly payments would be approximately $198 for 60 months. Does that sound manageable? 
Eleanor: Hmm, yeah, that's… okay. What are the fees like? 
Agent: There's a one-time origination fee of $200, and that's it. No prepayment penalties. We pride ourselves on transparency. 
Eleanor: Okay. And the interest rate is fixed at 8.99%? 
Agent: Absolutely. Fixed for the life of the loan. We can get the paperwork started right away if you'd like. 
Eleanor: Yeah, let's do it. 
Agent: Fantastic! I'm confident this Personal Loan will be a great fit for you. Just need a few more details to confirm…

Agent: Hello, Mr. Miller? This is Sarah calling from Finance Solutions. We're offering a special promotion on our Signature Credit Card. 
Robert: Where did you get my number? I didn't give anyone permission to call me. 
Agent: Sir, we… uh… it's a marketing list. We're offering a $2500 Signature Credit Card with an interest rate of 19.99%. 
Robert: 19.99?! Are you kidding me? That's outrageous! I already have a credit card. 
Agent: Well, sir, this card offers rewards points and… 
Robert: No, thank you. I'm not interested. And please, don't call me again. *click*

Agent: Hi Jessica, this is David from Finance Solutions. I'm calling regarding a Personal Loan. Your friend, Maria Rodriguez, referred you. 
Jessica: Oh, hi! Yeah, Maria mentioned you guys. I'm thinking about renovating my kitchen. 
Agent: That's fantastic! We can definitely help with that. We are pre-approving you for a $15,000 Personal Loan at a rate of 12.5%. The monthly payments would be around $330 for five years. 
Jessica: Okay, that sounds… reasonable. What about fees? 
Agent: There's a $350 appraisal fee, and a $100 document processing fee. We also have a small annual account maintenance fee of $50. 
Jessica: Hmm, okay. So, $350 for the appraisal… is that negotiable? 
Agent: Unfortunately, the appraisal fee is standard for loans of this size. It ensures we have an accurate assessment of the property. 
Jessica: I see. And the 12.5% is fixed? 
Agent: Yes, it's a fixed rate for the entire loan term. We can also discuss different loan terms to see if we can lower your monthly payment, but that might increase the total interest paid. 
Jessica: Let me think about it. Can you send me a detailed breakdown of all the fees and the loan terms in an email? 
Agent: Absolutely! I'll send that over right away. And feel free to reach out if you have any questions.

Agent: Hello, Mr. Brown? This is Lisa from Finance Solutions. We're offering a… 
Michael: (Interrupting) Look, you guys called me yesterday! I told you I'm not interested! I'm unemployed, okay? I don't need a Signature Credit Card with a ridiculous 24.99% interest rate! 
Agent: Sir, I understand your frustration, but… 
Michael: No, you don't understand! I'm struggling to make ends meet! Just take my number off your list! 
Agent: We can certainly… 
Michael: *click*

Agent: Good afternoon, Ms. Davis. This is Alex with Finance Solutions. I see you submitted an application for a Personal Loan online. 
Patricia: Oh, yes, hello. I'm looking to consolidate some debt. 
Agent: Excellent. We are pre-approving you for a $20,000 Personal Loan at a rate of 7.99%. As a valued existing customer, we're offering you our best rate. 
Patricia: That sounds good. What are the terms? 
Agent: We can offer terms from 3 to 7 years. A 7-year term would result in a monthly payment of approximately $315. 
Patricia: Hmm, that's a bit higher than I was hoping for. Could we look at a 5-year term? 
Agent: Certainly. A 5-year term would be around $385 per month. 
Patricia: Okay. And what about fees? I don't want any hidden surprises. 
Agent: There's a $250 origination fee, and a $75 document processing fee. No prepayment penalties. 
Patricia: That's reasonable. I'm also curious about your competitor's rates. I've been looking at First National Bank. 
Agent: First National Bank typically has higher rates for customers with credit scores below 800. And their origination fees are significantly higher, around $400. We're offering you a superior rate and lower fees. 
Patricia: That's good to know. I think I'm ready to proceed with the 5-year term. 
Agent: Wonderful! I'm confident you'll be very happy with this Personal Loan. Let's just verify a few details to finalize the application…
